idea: A Chrome extension that summarises long YouTube videos into 5 bullet points using AI.
notes: Crowded category; YouTube ships native AI summaries to Premium plus several funded extensions exist. No defensible wedge.

idea: A weekly digest service that emails immigration lawyers every USCIS policy change relevant to H-1B work, written in plain English, $39/mo.
notes: Sharp persona, recurring trigger, observable willingness to pay (AILA, Bloomberg Law). Wedge: price + tone + narrow scope.

idea: An AI app that generates dinner recipes from a photo of your fridge.
notes: Saturated and consumer; many free apps and ChatGPT itself do this. No defensible distribution for a solo founder.

idea: A todo list app with kanban boards.
notes: The most saturated category in software. No persona, no wedge.

idea: A SaaS that lets veterinary radiologists store and query their own past X-rays as a personal 'priors' database, $99/mo.
notes: Real persona and pain, narrow market — needs more validation on willingness to pay before committing.

idea: A tool that auto-generates an EPK (electronic press kit) website for indie musicians from their Spotify + Instagram, $15/mo.
notes: Clear persona, real pain, but Linktree/Bandcamp adjacency is risky. Needs sharper wedge before 'build'.

idea: A turnkey bus-tracker app for private K-12 schools in the US that don't have a transport software vendor, sold via parent-teacher groups.
notes: Specific persona and channel, but heavy implementation; verdict depends on whether schools will buy without a sales team.

idea: A new social network like Twitter but for [unspecified audience].
notes: Network effects + zero specificity. Always a kill.

idea: A checklist + client-portal SaaS for solo CPAs preparing US tax returns for self-employed clients, $59/mo, sold via r/taxpros.
notes: Sharp persona, recurring annual pain, named distribution channel, sub-$100 ACV solo founders can sell.

idea: A new smartwatch that tracks heart rate and steps better than Apple Watch.
notes: Capital-intensive hardware vs. trillion-dollar incumbent. Solo founder cannot ship this.

idea: A small SaaS that automates the post-shoot client follow-up sequence (gallery delivery, review request, referral ask) for solo wedding photographers, $25/mo.
notes: Specific persona, recurring revenue, named workflow, sub-$30 price founder can sell via Instagram + Pixieset community.
No persona, no wedge, no business model. Always kill at this fidelity.

idea: A Mac menu-bar app that turns voice memos from physiotherapists' patient sessions into structured SOAP notes for their EMR, HIPAA-aware, $39/mo.
notes: Strong persona and pain, but HIPAA + EMR integration risk. Iterate to scope down before build.

idea: A SaaS that audits an Etsy shop's listings for SEO and suggests title/tag improvements, $19/mo.
notes: Real persona, real pain, but several incumbents (eRank, Marmalead, Alura) own the keyword. Iterate to find a wedge.

idea: A mobile app that teaches Spanish to English speakers using gamification and streaks.
notes: Duolingo owns this. No solo-operator wedge.
